critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

1. DEMAND_RESPONSE_CONFOUND: Interruptible-load contracts and TOU tariff revision changed the load generation process between years. When peak loads are curtailed through interruptible contracts or shifted by tariffs, the load curve becomes flatter and more predictable. A flatter load curve with controlled peaks is genuinely easier to forecast. The MAPE reduction may reflect an easier target process, not a better model.

2. NONSTATIONARY_TARGET_PROCESS: The TOU tariff shifted commercial cooling load behavior. This is a structural demand-side change that alters the load curve shape. Year-over-year MAPE comparisons are valid only if the same underlying process is being predicted in both years. When the process itself changes, error statistics are not comparable across years.

3. WEATHER_INPUT_MISMATCH: New weather vendor with better station coverage means the new model receives higher-quality exogenous inputs. Part of the MAPE improvement may be attributable to better weather forecasts, not to the modeling ensemble choice.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE:

The new model is designed for and evaluated in the current operational environment. The interruptible-load programs and TOU tariffs are part of the grid it must forecast. A model that accurately forecasts load under demand-response conditions is more useful than one that would be accurate on the old unconstrained grid.

However, the specific claim — that the year-over-year improvement demonstrates better forecasting methodology — requires holding the environment constant. The demand-response programs, TOU tariff, and weather inputs all changed. The comparison is not between two methods applied to the same problem but between two methods applied to two different problems in two different years.

The new model may be better for the current grid conditions. Establishing this requires evaluating both models on the same historical data with the same weather inputs, isolating methodology from environmental and input changes.
adjudication_raw: Both parties converge: the year-over-year comparison is confounded by demand-response changes, process nonstationarity, and weather input differences. The new model may still be better for current grid conditions. Empirical_test_agreed: evaluate both models on matched historical intervals with identical weather inputs.

empirical_test.measure: Both models evaluated on same historical summer intervals with identical weather vendor data; MAPE separately on curtailment days vs. ordinary days
empirical_test.success_criterion: New model shows no consistent improvement on matched intervals — year-over-year gap reflects environmental change, not methodology
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New model consistently improves MAPE on matched intervals under identical weather inputs — confirming genuine methodology improvement
